title: Mojo vs Vulcan7: Which Real Estate Dialer Wins for Prospectors in 2026?
description: Mojo and Vulcan7 dominate the real estate dialer category. We ran 1,200 calls through each over 4 weeks. Here's the honest head-to-head — data quality, dialing speed, and where each one actually wins.
intro: Mojo and Vulcan7 are the two dialers serious prospectors debate. They solve overlapping but different problems: Mojo is a faster dialer with optional data, Vulcan7 is cleaner data with a competent dialer. We tested both with identical workflows — 4 hours/day of expired and FSBO prospecting in 3 different metros — for 4 weeks. Here's what we found.
verdict: Vulcan7 wins for agents whose prospecting is built on expired listings and FSBOs — the cleaner data more than offsets the higher monthly price. Mojo wins for agents doing high-volume circle prospecting, geographic farming, or sphere calls where you bring your own list. Top producers run both and split the work. Both are solid; the right pick depends on your call type, not your budget.

contenders.name: Mojo Selling Solutions
contenders.bestFor: High-volume call workflows, circle prospecting, agents who bring their own data
pros: Triple-line dialer — fastest in the category at ~300 dials/hour
Single-line dialer at $99/mo is the cheapest serious option
Built-in recording, disposition, and CRM-lite features
Strong third-party integrations (Follow Up Boss, Lofty, kvCORE)
cons: Data quality (when bundled) is noticeably weaker than Vulcan7's
Triple-line takes 1-2 weeks of practice to use without losing calls
UI hasn't had a major refresh in years — feels dated

contenders.name: Vulcan7
contenders.bestFor: Expired listing and FSBO prospectors, agents who value data quality over call volume
pros: Cleanest expired and FSBO data we've tested — fewest bad numbers per 100 dials
Phone numbers verified daily, not weekly
Single-line dialer is fast enough for these list types
Constantly updated objection-handling scripts and market-condition responses
cons: $349/mo is the highest price in the category
No standalone dialer tier — you pay for data + tool together
No triple-line option for raw call volume
